notes: Major XFS features merged into Linus's tree since 2009. RFC dates, LSFMM exposure, and enterprise distro enablement are hand-curated and carry source: provenance fields. Merge SHAs are seed commits identified from xfs feature bit definitions and topic-branch merge points; the pipeline's kernel_git.py derives merged_date, merged_version, and Fixes-tag counts automatically.

features.name: Delayed logging
features.description: Reworks XFS journaling to delay log buffer writes, batching changes and dramatically reducing journal I/O on metadata-heavy workloads. The replacement for the original transaction model and the foundation for everything that came after on the log.
features.suse_notes: Available in 3.0-based kernel; default for new XFS log behavior.
features.rhel_notes: RHEL 7 ships XFS as default and delaylog is enabled.
features.notes: Authored by Dave Chinner. Merged v2.6.35.

features.name: v5 superblock with CRC self-describing metadata
features.description: XFS_SB_VERSION_5 format with CRC32c protection on every metadata block, owner/UUID stamps, and self-describing block headers. Foundation for every later feature bit and required for online scrub/repair.
features.suse_notes: Supported but mkfs default became v5 only in SLE 15.
features.rhel_notes: Default mkfs in RHEL 7.3+; required for reflink in RHEL 8.
features.notes: Authored primarily by Dave Chinner. Merged v3.10.

features.name: Filetype in directory entries
features.description: INCOMPAT_FTYPE records inode type in directory entries to avoid readdir stat() back-references. Standard POSIX-quality readdir performance.
features.suse_notes: Available via v5 superblock; default became on with SLE 15.
features.rhel_notes: Default with v5 superblock in RHEL 7.3+.
features.notes: Merged v3.12.

features.name: Free inode btree (finobt)
features.description: RO_COMPAT_FINOBT adds a btree of free inode chunks distinct from the inode btree, dramatically accelerating inode allocation on aged filesystems.
features.notes: Authored by Brian Foster. Merged v3.16.

features.name: DAX support
features.description: Direct access to persistent memory bypassing the page cache. Required substantial mm changes for devmap pages and per-inode DAX state.
features.suse_notes: Available as mount option, not default.
features.rhel_notes: DAX support shipped as Technology Preview in RHEL 7.3, supported for ext4/XFS in 7.6, then deprecated and dropped in later RHEL.
features.mm_impact_notes: Required devmap pages, pgmap accounting, dax_iomap path, and many mm/ hooks. The first feature to drive multi-year mm refactors on behalf of fs/.
features.notes: XFS DAX support merged v4.2.

features.name: Sparse inodes
features.description: INCOMPAT_SPINODES allows inode chunks to be sparse rather than fixed 64-inode aligned, dramatically improving inode allocation on small or fragmented filesystems.
features.notes: Authored by Brian Foster. Merged v4.2.

features.name: Metadata UUID
features.description: INCOMPAT_META_UUID separates the UUID stamped in metadata blocks from the user-visible UUID, allowing UUID change without rewriting metadata.
features.notes: Merged v4.3.

features.name: Reverse mapping btree (rmapbt)
features.description: RO_COMPAT_RMAPBT records the owner of every allocated extent, enabling reflink, online repair, and forward/reverse extent walking.
features.suse_notes: mkfs option, off by default. Required for reflink.
features.rhel_notes: Available in RHEL 8.0, not default mkfs. Default on in RHEL 9.
features.notes: Authored by Darrick Wong. Merged v4.8 as experimental.

features.name: Reflinks (shared data extents)
features.description: RO_COMPAT_REFLINK adds CoW shared data extents using refcount btrees, enabling cp --reflink and dedup. The user-visible payoff of rmapbt.
features.suse_notes: Supported and mkfs option exposed; default became on later.
features.mm_impact_notes: Touched the iomap/CoW write path; no broad mm changes.
features.notes: Authored by Darrick Wong. Merged v4.9 as experimental, declared stable in v4.16.

features.name: Online scrub
features.description: XFS_IOC_SCRUB_METADATA ioctl plus the in-kernel scrub infrastructure that validates metadata consistency on a live filesystem.
features.rhel_notes: xfs_scrub shipped as Technology Preview, then supported.
features.notes: Authored by Darrick Wong. Merged v4.15 as experimental.

features.name: pNFS layout
features.description: Block-layout pNFS server export operations for XFS, enabling clients to perform direct device I/O bypassing the NFS data server.
features.rhel_notes: Not exposed as a supported configuration.
features.notes: Authored by Christoph Hellwig. Merged v4.0.

features.name: Swap files
features.description: swap_activate via iomap, finally allowing /swapfile on XFS without bmap quirks.
features.mm_impact_notes: Touched swap subsystem to consume iomap-described extents.
features.notes: Authored by Darrick Wong. Merged v4.18.

features.name: iomap buffered I/O conversion
features.description: XFS writeback path lifted to iomap, dropping the buffer_head dependency on the buffered I/O path. Foundation for large folios in the page cache and LBS.
features.mm_impact_notes: Required page-cache awareness of iomap-typed writeback; later prerequisite for large folios.
features.notes: Authored by Christoph Hellwig and Darrick Wong. Merged v5.5.

features.name: Year-2038-safe timestamps (bigtime)
features.description: INCOMPAT_BIGTIME extends the on-disk timestamp epoch beyond 2038 by reinterpreting the seconds field as unsigned nanoseconds since 1901.
features.notes: Authored by Darrick Wong. Merged v5.10.

features.name: Inobt block count (inobtcount)
features.description: RO_COMPAT_INOBTCNT caches inode-btree block counts in the AGI header to speed up mount-time AG header parsing.
features.notes: Authored by Darrick Wong. Merged v5.10.

features.name: Needs-repair feature bit
features.description: INCOMPAT_NEEDSREPAIR marks a filesystem as requiring xfs_repair before further use, used by online repair to fence partially-repaired metadata.
features.notes: Authored by Darrick Wong. Merged v5.11.

features.name: Online repair
features.description: In-kernel metadata repair distinct from scrub: actually fixes detected damage on a live filesystem.
features.suse_notes: Experimental for years; targeting full enablement in 6.12-based releases.
features.notes: Authored by Darrick Wong. Initial repair ioctl flag merged v4.18; many years of iteration since.

features.name: Delayed attributes (log_xattrs)
features.description: INCOMPAT_LOG_XATTRS adds delayed attribute changes journaled through intent logging, removing the need for the old transaction-roll dance for xattr updates and unblocking parent pointers and online repair.
features.notes: Authored by Allison Henderson. Merged v5.19.

features.name: Large extent counts (nrext64)
features.description: INCOMPAT_NREXT64 widens on-disk extent count fields to 64 bits, removing the 4 billion data extent limit per inode.
features.notes: Authored by Chandan Babu R. Merged v5.19.

features.name: Atomic exchange-range
features.description: INCOMPAT_EXCHRANGE adds an atomic file range exchange operation, the building block for xfs_io exchangerange and metadirectory swap, plus the file-side of atomic writes.
features.mm_impact_notes: Required iomap atomic-write support and folio batch ranges.
features.notes: Authored by Darrick Wong. Merged v6.10.

features.name: Parent pointers
features.description: INCOMPAT_PARENT stores parent directory inode references in extended attributes, making path reconstruction and online repair tractable without scrubbing the directory tree.
features.notes: Authored by Allison Henderson with help from Darrick Wong. Merged v6.10.

features.name: Metadata directory tree
features.description: INCOMPAT_METADIR moves quota, rt summary, and rt bitmap inodes into a dedicated metadata directory tree, unblocking realtime groups and metadata-only btree types.
features.notes: Authored by Darrick Wong. Merged v6.13.

features.name: Zoned realtime allocator
features.description: INCOMPAT_ZONED arranges the realtime device into zones for SMR/ZNS devices, with garbage collection and per-zone metadata.
features.mm_impact_notes: Required iomap/zone interaction; no broad mm changes.
features.notes: Authored by Christoph Hellwig. Merged v6.15.

features.name: Large Block Sizes (LBS)
features.description: Filesystem block size larger than PAGE_SIZE on XFS, enabled by a multi-year stack of mm and iomap changes: large folios, mapping_set_folio_min_order, FS_LBS gating, and an LBS-aware block layer. The first XFS feature whose schedule visibly accelerated under kdevops-driven automated testing.
features.first_idea_note: Christoph Lameter's compound-page RFC for >PAGE_SIZE block sizes in the page cache.

historical_attempts.author: Christoph Lameter
historical_attempts.note: Compound-page support for large block sizes in the page cache. First serious proposal.

historical_attempts.author: Nick Piggin
historical_attempts.note: fsblock RFC to replace buffer heads. Alternative path to LBS, stalled.

historical_attempts.author: Nick Piggin
historical_attempts.note: fsblock updated patches. Did not land.

historical_attempts.author: LSFMM 2017
historical_attempts.note: LSFMM discussion on whether LBS was worth revisiting.

historical_attempts.author: Dave Chinner
historical_attempts.note: Renewed attempt at >PAGE_SIZE block sizes. Stalled.

features.merge_note: XFS LBS support landed v6.12 (commit 7df7c204c678 'xfs: enable block size larger than page size support', 2024-09-03). FS_LBS gating flag for the VFS added in v6.15 (a64e5a596067, 2025-03-07). Promoted from experimental together with metadirectory in v7.0 (4d6d335ea955, 2026-01-21).
features.suse_notes: Supported as opt-in mkfs option in SUSE 16 (6.12 base).
features.rhel_notes: Available in 6.12-based RHEL 10 as a Technology Preview / opt-in.
features.mm_impact_notes: Forced multi-year mm refactor: large folios in the page cache, mapping_set_folio_min_order, AS_FOLIO_ORDER_*, iomap large-folio writeback, and an LBS-aware block layer. By volume of mm/ touched, the largest fs-driven mm change in years.
features.notes: Authored by Pankaj Raghav, Hannes Reinecke, Luis Chamberlain, Matthew Wilcox, Dave Chinner, Darrick Wong and others. Test cycle was driven by kdevops with AI-assisted triage.
